jokes.text: We sent Elton and David a honey-baked ham. What more do they want?
jokes.setup: NBC trying to apologize to the gay community after Tracy's offensive comments
jokes.punchline: Thinking a honey-baked ham is appropriate compensation for homophobia
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack Donaghy - corporate executive completely out of touch with how to handle a PR crisis. The specificity of 'honey-baked ham' makes it funnier than generic gift.

jokes.text: I have seen you since Michael Kors' new year's Eve masquerade. Uh, I don't know what you're talking about.
jokes.setup: Live TV interview with gay rights advocate
jokes.punchline: Devin references a very gay-coded social event then host awkwardly denies knowing about it
jokes.explanation: Good awkward TV moment showing the host's discomfort with gay references. Sets up the tension between Devin and straight corporate world.

jokes.text: Yesterday, that was the face, feet and colon of a pig.
jokes.setup: Talking about fresh hot dogs
jokes.punchline: Gruesome but accurate description of hot dog ingredients
jokes.explanation: Dark but funny take on processed food. The specificity of 'face, feet and colon' elevates it beyond generic gross-out humor.

jokes.text: All right, kids. Who wants some? Get in my van.
jokes.setup: Criss trying to sell hot dogs to children
jokes.punchline: Accidentally sounds like a predator luring children
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy gold - the innocent hot dog vendor accidentally using predator language. Maximum impact with minimum words.

jokes.text: Is there a problem, officer?
jokes.setup: Criss just said 'get in my van' to children
jokes.punchline: Police immediately show up, confirming how suspicious he sounded
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the previous joke. The timing suggests police arrived immediately after his suspicious comment.

jokes.text: locally sourced pig sweepings
jokes.setup: Describing Criss's hot dog ingredients
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic description that sounds artisanal but means garbage
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay mocking foodie culture. 'Pig sweepings' sounds both artisanal and disgusting simultaneously.

jokes.text: Well, that's what she said, but...
jokes.setup: Liz mentions Jack inserting himself into things
jokes.punchline: Criss starts the 'that's what she said' joke but stops himself
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about recognizing but restraining himself from the obvious sexual innuendo. Shows self-awareness of the joke.

jokes.text: Wesleyan is the Harvard of central Connecticut. Yale is the Harvard of central Connecticut.
jokes.setup: Criss defending his college choice
jokes.punchline: Liz immediately deflates his comparison with a better one
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline structure. Shows Liz's intellectual superiority and Criss's pretensions. The rhythm is impeccable.

jokes.text: Had that locked and loaded, didn't you?
jokes.setup: Liz immediately countered his Wesleyan comparison
jokes.punchline: Criss realizes she was waiting to deploy that comeback
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Criss recognizing Liz's intellectual competitiveness. Natural reaction to her quick comeback.

jokes.text: You don't say cholesterol correctly. 'Chloresterole.'
jokes.setup: Fight between Liz and Criss escalating
jokes.punchline: Petty but accurate criticism of Liz's pronunciation
jokes.explanation: Specific character quirk for Liz. The phonetic spelling makes the mispronunciation clear and funnier.

jokes.text: You don't use the tab closures on cereal boxes. If you think those are doing anything to seal in freshness, you are living in a fantasy world, pal.
jokes.setup: Petty relationship fight continuing
jokes.punchline: Liz gets weirdly aggressive about cereal box design
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - Liz getting unreasonably passionate about cereal box tabs. Perfect character moment showing her neuroses.

jokes.text: What would Jack say about that? He'd say, 'good God, Lemon.' Locked and loaded.
jokes.setup: Criss going to meditate in the park
jokes.punchline: Immediately predicting Jack's response, using the 'locked and loaded' phrase from earlier
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier 'locked and loaded' line while also showing how predictable Jack is. Criss is learning to anticipate Jack's responses.

jokes.text: Good God, Lemon.
jokes.setup: Criss just predicted Jack would say this exact phrase
jokes.punchline: Jack immediately says it, confirming how predictable he is
jokes.explanation: Perfect execution of the setup from previous scene. Jack's predictability becomes the punchline. Flawless timing and economy.

jokes.text: I'll have you know the last man who wore jeans in this office was named Theodore Wrangler. And you, Banks, could not even carry his trademark, floor-length, suede cape.
jokes.setup: Jack criticizing Devin's casual attire
jokes.punchline: Absurd story about a previous jeans-wearer with an even more absurd fashion choice
jokes.explanation: Peak 30 Rock absurdism. The name 'Theodore Wrangler' and 'floor-length suede cape' are perfectly ridiculous details that feel like they could be real in this universe.

jokes.text: Do you know what comes up on my phone when you call? [Shows photo of Jack in compromising position]
jokes.setup: Devin threatening Jack with leverage
jokes.punchline: Revealing embarrassing photo of Jack on his phone contact
jokes.explanation: Standard visual gag setup. The photo itself is the punchline but we don't see it clearly, reducing impact.

jokes.text: The only copy of that photograph is in Stephanie Seymour's safety deposit box.
jokes.setup: Jack seeing compromising photo of himself
jokes.punchline: Implies sexual relationship with supermodel and elaborate photo security
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jack's complicated romantic history and paranoia. Stephanie Seymour reference dates the show but fits Jack's character.

jokes.text: We're new, we're called PEEN. And what is that an acronym for? Acronym?
jokes.setup: Devin claiming to represent an organization
jokes.punchline: Organization name sounds sexual and he doesn't know what acronym means
jokes.explanation: Juvenile penis joke elevated by Devin's confusion about 'acronym.' Shows his schemes are hastily planned.

jokes.text: The highest grossing movie of all time! The boat, not the movie, the boat!
jokes.setup: Jack getting excited when Devin mentions Titanic
jokes.punchline: Jack thinks of financial success first, then remembers the actual disaster
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack moment - his corporate mindset makes him think of box office before tragedy. The desperate correction is perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: Then she had teeth down there!
jokes.setup: Playing Tracy's offensive standup routine
jokes.punchline: Bizarre and disturbing sexual imagery
jokes.explanation: Shock humor without much craft. Included to show how offensive Tracy's material is, but not particularly clever on its own.

jokes.text: All right, you can watch me shower, but no touching. No touching just makes it hotter.
jokes.setup: Jack asking what Devin wants for his silence
jokes.punchline: Jack assumes sexual blackmail, Devin confirms the appeal
jokes.explanation: Good setup/punchline about their complicated relationship. Jack's assumption and Devin's confirmation create sexual tension humor.

jokes.text: I want you to get my triplets into preschool... At St. Matthew's.
jokes.setup: Build up to Devin's big demand after all the sexual innuendo
jokes.punchline: Anticlimactic request for preschool admission
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - after sexual buildup, he wants help with elite preschool. Shows how competitive NYC parenting has become.

jokes.text: I head that during her interview she turned the class hamster into a dove.
jokes.setup: Discussing rejected preschool applicant who was St. Matthew's descendant
jokes.punchline: Child performed literal miracle and still got rejected
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation of elite preschool competition. If a child who can perform miracles gets rejected, the standards are impossibly high.

jokes.text: We should take two cars. You didn't tell me he was alive! Well of course he is. Pete's our friend, Kelsey.
jokes.setup: Conversation that sounds like body disposal
jokes.punchline: They're just helping their friend Pete, not committing murder
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection making innocent conversation sound sinister. The casual 'of course he is' deflates the murder implications.

jokes.text: Damn it! No names!
jokes.setup: Kelsey using Pete's name in what sounds like criminal activity
jokes.punchline: Grammer panicking about operational security for innocent activity
jokes.explanation: Kelsey Grammer treating innocent situation like serious crime. His dramatic actor intensity applied to mundane situation creates comedy.

jokes.text: His office is on this floor, isn't it? Yes, but to get him there, you have to go through the backstage and the backstage is full of people.
jokes.setup: Planning to help Pete get to his office
jokes.punchline: Treating the building like a complex infiltration mission
jokes.explanation: Continues the absurd spy movie treatment of mundane office navigation. Shows how they're overcomplicating simple problems.

jokes.text: How long is the show? I don't know. I've never done it before, baby.
jokes.setup: Asking about timing for their distraction plan
jokes.punchline: Kelsey admits he's never performed his Lincoln show
jokes.explanation: Reveals Kelsey's Lincoln performance is improvised. His calling Kenneth 'baby' adds to his eccentric actor persona.

jokes.text: Since its founding early this afternoon, the national association for zero intolerance, or Nazi... We should change that.
jokes.setup: Tracy announcing his new organization
jokes.punchline: Acronym accidentally spells 'Nazi' which is opposite of their intent
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic acronym failure. An anti-intolerance organization accidentally naming itself after the ultimate intolerant group is brilliantly absurd.

jokes.text: It's fine.
jokes.setup: Assistant suggesting they change the Nazi acronym
jokes.punchline: Tracy casually dismissing concern about being called Nazi
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy response - completely missing why 'Nazi' would be problematic. Two words that perfectly capture his obliviousness.

jokes.text: My ringtone is the chicken dance. If I answer it, I won't hear the whole song!
jokes.setup: Liz saying she's been calling Tracy all day
jokes.punchline: Tracy's ridiculous reason for not answering his phone
jokes.explanation: Pure Tracy logic - caring more about hearing his novelty ringtone than taking important calls. Perfectly absurd character reasoning.

jokes.text: These microphones look like black ice cream cones.
jokes.setup: Press conference with Tracy at podium
jokes.punchline: Tracy distracted by microphone appearance during serious moment
jokes.explanation: Classic Tracy - getting distracted by random observations during important events. Shows his child-like perspective on the world.

jokes.text: You can't tear up my checks, Criss. They're printed on Nixon's old bed sheets.
jokes.setup: Criss trying to return Jack's money
jokes.punchline: Jack's checks are made from bizarre historical artifacts
jokes.explanation: Peak Jack absurdity - of course his checks would be printed on presidential bedding. Shows his wealth and eccentricity perfectly.

jokes.text: She's my subordifriend.
jokes.setup: Jack explaining his relationship with Liz
jokes.punchline: Made-up word combining 'subordinate' and 'friend'
jokes.explanation: Clever portmanteau that perfectly captures Jack's complex relationship with Liz. Corporate but affectionate.

jokes.text: Damn it, Criss, stop talking to him, it's a trap! You said I won.
jokes.setup: Jack manipulating the Liz/Criss relationship
jokes.punchline: Liz realizes Jack tricked her into bringing Criss over
jokes.explanation: Shows Liz's awareness of Jack's manipulative patterns. Her frustration at being outmaneuvered again is relatable.

jokes.text: Semantics!
jokes.setup: Jack pointing out he technically kept his word
jokes.punchline: Liz's frustrated academic response
jokes.explanation: Liz's writer background showing in frustrated intellectual response

jokes.text: The basket of shells was a nice touch.
jokes.setup: Jack rearranged Liz's bathroom
jokes.punchline: Liz grudgingly admits his improvements were good
jokes.explanation: Shows Liz's conflicted feelings - Jack violated her space but improved it. The specific 'basket of shells' detail makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I go back to sleeping on the floor of my Uncle's mattress store
jokes.setup: Criss describing his living situation if they break up
jokes.punchline: He sleeps on the floor despite being surrounded by mattresses
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic situation - homeless in a mattress store but still sleeping on the floor. Shows Criss's pathetic circumstances.

jokes.text: Well, I have to go get a bunch of gaybies into preschool before we all get sued
jokes.setup: Jack leaving after manipulating Liz's relationship
jokes.punchline: Casual use of 'gaybies' to describe Devin's children
jokes.explanation: Jack's casual portmanteau while treating serious situations as business problems. Shows his compartmentalization of personal issues.

jokes.text: I'll tell you, friendship is the one kind of ship that never sinks.
jokes.setup: Jack schmoozing at the club
jokes.punchline: Corny wordplay about friendship and ships
jokes.explanation: Deliberately cheesy dad joke that Jack would use for networking. The corniness is the point but it's not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Although, during my financial report I may be President of the bored.
jokes.setup: Douglas discussing his role at St. Matthew's
jokes.punchline: Self-aware pun about boring financial reports
jokes.explanation: Standard dad joke from rich guy character. Self-deprecating but not particularly original or clever.

jokes.text: Hey, you. Hey, funny.
jokes.setup: Jack greeting Don Geiss's daughter
jokes.punchline: She responds with childlike, simple greetings
jokes.explanation: Shows Kathy's childlike nature despite inheriting a fortune. The simple exchange reveals character dynamics efficiently.

jokes.text: Magellica the Unicorn. It's wondrous.
jokes.setup: NBC introducing new mascot to replace controversy
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous unicorn mascot with pretentious name
jokes.explanation: Corporate absurdity - replacing controversy with magical mascot. 'Magellica' sounds both fancy and ridiculous.

jokes.text: I played a 'nucular' psychiatrist in a James 'Bong' movie.
jokes.setup: Tracy defending idiot community achievements
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces 'nuclear' and 'Bond' while boasting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy moment - trying to sound sophisticated while mispronouncing everything. The irony of defending idiots while proving the point.

jokes.text: ♪ La piscine ♪ j'adore la piscine ♪ towels sunscreen bathing suits ♪ diving boards and towels ♪ those ladders ♪ towels
jokes.setup: Tracy's music video playing during negotiation
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous French song about pools with repetitive English words
jokes.explanation: Absurd Tracy Jordan song with pretentious French mixed with mundane pool items. The repetition of 'towels' is particularly silly.

jokes.text: I couldn't find a Lincoln hat so I grabbed this chimney sweep hat instead. If anyone notices... We're dead.
jokes.setup: Kelsey preparing for Lincoln performance
jokes.punchline: Wrong period hat creates life-or-death stakes in his mind
jokes.explanation: Grammer's dramatic actor intensity applied to costume details. Treating hat accuracy as matter of life and death is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Idiots aren't just strippers or stay at home moms.
jokes.setup: Tracy defending the idiot community
jokes.punchline: Accidentally insults strippers and stay-at-home moms while trying to be inclusive
jokes.explanation: Tracy's attempt at inclusive message backfires by stereotyping other groups. Shows his good intentions with poor execution.

jokes.text: All the favors that you called in, you would have used next year for little lippy. Liddy. Is that even a name?
jokes.setup: Devin explaining how Jack used up his social capital
jokes.punchline: Keeps mispronouncing and mocking Liddy's name
jokes.explanation: Good character animosity through name mockery. Devin's petty attacks on Jack's parenting choices feel personal and cruel.

jokes.text: I wonder what Liddy will be doing with her degree from suny. Don't even say it. Oneonta.
jokes.setup: Devin predicting Liddy's educational future
jokes.punchline: Mentions specific SUNY campus as ultimate failure
jokes.explanation: Specific college reference that's funnier for knowing SUNY Oneonta's reputation. Jack's horror at the specific campus name adds to the elitism.

jokes.text: Yeah, I guess you're right.
jokes.setup: Kenneth wearing wrong historical hat
jokes.punchline: Immediate agreement suggests visual is obviously wrong
jokes.explanation: Simple visual payoff to earlier setup

jokes.text: I used an offensive term to describe a group of people who made America the great... continent that it is today.
jokes.setup: Liz giving apology for offensive comments
jokes.punchline: Calls America a continent instead of country
jokes.explanation: Liz's geographic error during formal apology undermines her credibility. Shows how her contempt for idiots makes her sloppy too.

jokes.text: Water parks... no, I will not endorse water parks. They are a cesspool of disease and people boo you when you walk back down the stairs.
jokes.setup: Liz listing things idiots gave the world
jokes.punchline: Gets sidetracked by personal water park grievance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz tangent - her fake speech derailed by personal water park trauma. The specific image of being booed on stairs is perfectly pathetic.

jokes.text: Oh, I get it. She's naming awesome things. No, listen to me. Because of you, there may be an entourage movie.
jokes.setup: Audience member misunderstanding Liz's sarcastic list
jokes.punchline: Liz clarifies with threat of Entourage movie as ultimate horror
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing how audience misses sarcasm. Entourage movie as cultural apocalypse was timely and specific fear.

jokes.text: Hug it off, ma'am! Turtle, turtle, turtle!
jokes.setup: Crowd still not understanding Liz's criticism
jokes.punchline: Entourage references proving Liz's point about lowbrow culture
jokes.explanation: Crowd proving Liz's point by quoting the exact show she's criticizing. The repetitive 'turtle' chant is appropriately mindless.

jokes.text: You will make the same self-destructive decisions over and over and you will never really be happy. And your jaw will hurt all the time.
jokes.setup: Liz's rant against idiots reaching climax
jokes.punchline: Ends with oddly specific physical complaint about jaw pain
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending to Liz's rant - from existential despair to random physical complaint. The jaw pain detail is wonderfully specific and petty.

jokes.text: Perfect little weiner dog, you just need a little mustard on you like that. And a little bun, just boop, like that.
jokes.setup: Liz breaking down during her rant
jokes.punchline: Starts treating person like actual hot dog with condiments
jokes.explanation: Liz's complete breakdown manifesting as hot dog fantasy. The 'boop' sound effect and physical comedy make this memorably weird.

jokes.text: No one will make you deutschepancakes, or welcome you home smelling of hot dog water and onion, or let you break out of jail when you play monopoly.
jokes.setup: Liz's breakdown continuing
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly specific and pathetic romantic gestures
jokes.explanation: Beautiful character moment showing Liz's romantic longing through absurdly specific details. Each item gets more pathetic and endearing.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, I'm such an idiot.
jokes.setup: Liz realizes she's been criticizing idiots
jokes.punchline: Admits she's one herself, completing the ironic circle
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic reversal - Liz's anti-idiot crusade ends with self-recognition. Excellent structure and character development in one line.

jokes.text: It's funny, in school all you learn about Abraham Lincoln is that he was a gay alcoholic.
jokes.setup: Kelsey backstage during Lincoln performance
jokes.punchline: Completely incorrect historical facts delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd historical misinformation delivered with confidence. Kelsey's casual wrongness about basic history is perfectly ridiculous.

jokes.text: Hit me in the face!
jokes.setup: Kelsey preparing to return to Lincoln performance
jokes.punchline: Requests violence to get into character
jokes.explanation: Method actor intensity applied to historical performance. Grammer's commitment to getting slapped for authenticity is absurdly dedicated.

jokes.text: In a lot of ways, you and I had similar upbringings. Terrible schools, broken homes... Thinking basketball was the ticket out. Being wrong.
jokes.setup: Tracy comparing himself to Jack
jokes.punchline: Lists shared struggles that completely don't apply to wealthy Jack
jokes.explanation: Great character blindness - Tracy assuming Jack shares his background. The basketball detail is particularly off-base for elite Jack.

jokes.text: I said creating a movement.
jokes.setup: Tracy mentioning he spent the day 'creating a movement'
jokes.punchline: Clarifies he means political movement, not bowel movement
jokes.explanation: Basic bathroom humor pun. Tracy recognizing the double entendre is character-appropriate but the joke itself is elementary.

jokes.text: In this country, anyone could be the next Jack Donaghy or the next Denise Richards.
jokes.setup: Tracy making point about American opportunity
jokes.punchline: Pairs successful executive with actress known for bad movies
jokes.explanation: Good pairing of success with failure as equal American dreams. Denise Richards as exemplar of failure is dated but effective.

jokes.text: This room... Is moving.
jokes.setup: Tracy in the elevator
jokes.punchline: Discovers elevators move, showing his basic lack of awareness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy moment - amazed by basic technology. His wonder at elevator movement shows his child-like perspective on the world.

jokes.text: I had the best education in the world. Private kindergarten where I got straight check marks.
jokes.setup: Devin describing his privileged education
jokes.punchline: Boasts about kindergarten grades that don't exist
jokes.explanation: Perfect satire of elite education obsession. Bragging about kindergarten performance is absurd, and 'check marks' aren't grades.

jokes.text: Experimental boarding school in carmel where the students teach the teachers, and the teachers teach animals.
jokes.setup: Continuing Devin's educational pedigree
jokes.punchline: Progressively more ridiculous experimental education
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of progressive education gone mad. The hierarchy of students-teachers-animals teaching each other is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Then on to Northwestern where I majored in confidence.
jokes.setup: Completing educational background
jokes.punchline: College major that doesn't exist but perfectly describes his personality
jokes.explanation: Perfect capstone to Devin's absurd education. 'Majored in confidence' explains his personality while being completely ridiculous.

jokes.text: She's already sorting objects by shape and color. At 11 months, I don't think so. She can count to ten if you say seven for her.
jokes.setup: Jack bragging about baby Liddy's abilities
jokes.punchline: Reveals the counting is mostly him helping her
jokes.explanation: Great parenting competition satire. Jack's defensive explanation that he helps with counting undermines his bragging perfectly.

jokes.text: She gets five and nine? She can save five words. So. In mandarin.
jokes.setup: Competitive parenting continuing
jokes.punchline: Jack claiming baby speaks Chinese
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of competitive parenting. Jack's casual claim about Mandarin is perfectly absurd for an 11-month-old.

jokes.text: She scored a 62 on the object permanence matrix. But that's an adult score.
jokes.setup: Jack's ultimate baby bragging claim
jokes.punchline: Made-up test with impossible results
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of competitive parenting to absurd scientific claims. 'Object permanence matrix' sounds real but is completely ridiculous.

jokes.text: And did I mention... She's using the potty. Surely just for wee-wee. No.
jokes.setup: Jack's final parenting triumph
jokes.punchline: Baby doing something genuinely impossible for her age
jokes.explanation: Ultimate parenting competition victory through bathroom humor. The pause and 'No' creates perfect dramatic timing for the reveal.

jokes.text: Oh, it's burning the backs of my eyes. Oh, it's burning my face now.
jokes.setup: Criss getting mustard up his nose
jokes.punchline: Mustard causing escalating physical pain
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy with mustard. Functional but not particularly clever or memorable compared to the show's usual standards.

jokes.text: Kelsey Grammer! Kelsey Grammer did this to me.
jokes.setup: Pete found in compromising position
jokes.punchline: Blames respected actor for his predicament
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the Pete mystery plot. Using Kelsey Grammer's real name adds to the absurdity of the situation.

jokes.text: Really, Pete? Kelsey Grammer made you do this while he was performing his one man show about Abraham Lincoln?
jokes.setup: Pete's excuse about Kelsey Grammer
jokes.punchline: Liz's sarcastic disbelief at the absurd explanation
jokes.explanation: Liz's incredulity at Pete's ridiculous excuse. Her sarcastic tone makes the absurd situation even funnier.

jokes.text: I look incredibly beautiful. The lighting is back to normal. Both switches need to be on, woman.
jokes.setup: Kenneth noticing he looks good again
jokes.punchline: Reveals simple solution to lighting problem they overcomplicated
jokes.explanation: Good revelation that their elaborate schemes were unnecessary. Kenneth's vanity and simple solution create nice payoff.

jokes.text: The bulb wasn't even broken? None of this was necessary.
jokes.setup: Realizing all their scheming was pointless
jokes.punchline: The entire episode's crisis was based on misunderstanding
jokes.explanation: Perfect sitcom revelation that the entire plot was unnecessary. Classic comedy structure with efficient execution.

jokes.text: So that's where that name came from.
jokes.setup: Lincoln mentioning civil war
jokes.punchline: Acts like he just figured out why it's called Civil War
jokes.explanation: Great meta-joke about historical terminology. Lincoln having an 'aha moment' about his own war's name is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Where everybody knows your name.
jokes.setup: Lincoln's vision of future America
jokes.punchline: References Cheers theme song (Kelsey Grammer's other famous role)
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke using Kelsey Grammer's Cheers connection. Lincoln prophesying the Cheers theme song is perfect 30 Rock absurdity and actor awareness.
